message.content:
Test file: src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Failing test: "should bring dead cell to life with exactly 3 live neighbors (reproduction)"
Expected behavior: Dead cell with exactly 3 live neighbors becomes alive. Given cells [(0,0),(1,0),(0,1)] the dead cell (1,1) has exactly 3 live neighbors and should appear in the next generation.
Current error: Expected { x: 1, y: 1 }, received [ { x: 1, y: 0 } ] - reproduction not implemented, only survival is handled
Implementation file: src/game-of-life.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: Let me read the current implementation and test files to understand what needs to be changed.

The test expects that dead cell (1,1) appears in the next generation when it has exactly 3 live neighbors. The current implementation only filters existing live cells (survival), but doesn't add dead cells that should come alive (reproduction).

The minimal implementation needs to also find dead cells adjacent to live cells and bring them to life if they have exactly 3 live neighbors.

content.text:
All 7 tests pass (2 are still `todo`).

Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Added `getCandidateDeadCells` helper that collects all dead cells neighboring at least one live cell, then filters those with exactly 3 live neighbors as newborns. The `nextGeneration` function now returns both survivors and newborns.

**Result**: All tests now pass (7 passing, 2 todo)

**Approach**: This is the minimal implementation needed - the test demands reproduction logic, so we had to add it. The helper gathers candidate dead cells (neighbors of live cells) and applies the reproduction rule (exactly 3 live neighbors). No future features added, no optimization.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.

**Implementation file**: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-14_21-09-13_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_sonnet-4-6-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts`
